codeReview.summary: The reviewed feature is not actually implemented by commit 6b3496e28f9d5ffa55fcb750db99f963d2197d9e. The commit only adds crypto configuration metadata and default-config APIs; runtime auto-registration/auto-ensure is still absent, and Store/runtime-resolver paths still require pre-registered crypto contexts.

issues.description: resolve_crypto_context only reads from the manual resolver registry and returns CryptoError::ResolverNotFound when no mapping was pre-registered. There is no lazy default-provider initialization, no OnceLock-backed per-model reuse, and no code here that consumes SensitiveFieldMetadata plus global defaults to auto-create a CryptoContext.

The generated encrypt_with_runtime_resolver/decrypt_with_runtime_resolver implementations directly call the runtime resolver for each field but do not auto-ensure readiness first. Because no generated OnceLock/ensure_crypto_ready() path exists, direct runtime-resolver operations still fail unless the caller has already registered contexts manually.

The runtime test suite still asserts that calling encrypt_with_runtime_resolver without prior registration returns CryptoError::ResolverNotFound. This contradicts the feature contract that direct runtime-resolver paths should auto-ensure readiness using defaults/overrides.

Integration coverage still depends on install_sensitive_test_contexts() to pre-register crypto contexts before Store operations. The reviewed tests verify manual setup works; they do not verify the promised behavior that save/get/list/save_many succeed without business-layer registration.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked the feature successful without checking that the cited commit actually contained runtime auto-registration behavior. The transcript shows a verification-only review of inherited code, but the inspected source still matches the prerequisite config-surface implementation rather than the requested runtime feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff commitId is 6b3496e28f9d5ffa55fcb750db99f963d2197d9e, and git show --stat for that commit reports only the commit header with no file changes. Current source still has ResolverNotFound behavior in core/src/crypto.rs and the failing-without-registration test in core/tests/sensitive_roundtrip.rs:193.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission/shared-state docs describe auto crypto readiness as the intended mainline pattern, but the current test helpers still normalize manual registration through install_sensitive_test_contexts(). That can mislead workers into treating the manual path as sufficient verification for auto-registration milestones.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission AGENTS/environment/library docs all state automatic crypto readiness is the goal, while core/tests/integration_db.rs:567 centralizes manual register_crypto_context_for setup and the auto-registration feature transcript cites those tests as proof.

summary: Fail: the reviewed feature claim is unsupported. The referenced commit does not implement runtime auto-registration, direct runtime-resolver paths still fail without manual registration, and Store integration tests still rely on preinstalled crypto contexts instead of validating no-registration behavior.
